author: @desireeactionz
text: Went 2 weeks ago and very impressed with the amazing beauty that Jiuzhaigou has to offer! The weather was pretty cloudy and rainy as well but the waters looked so pretty! The Five Colour Pond that I saw had a lot lesser water than what was shown in the video though hahaha

text: how many hours do you think we need to trek the western route only from arrow bamboo lake to nourilang and back to exit?

author: @lingesar
text: I was there in 2007 and after that the earthquake closed the place for a few years and it was great to see this place again through your video. I will visit again for sure. The buses are not arranged properly and not very efficient. They should plough a fixed route for anyone to get on or off the buss as they wish.  Other than that everything is 100 %. Disney could advise them on this matter. The public toilets was invented by a Taiwanese.

author: @Wy9083
text: I just visited it 2 weeks ago. We spent 2 solid days which is very worthwhile.

author: @Faith_in_the_cqmu
text:
Hiiiii sis , I really loved how you captured all the lakes really beautifully ❤ And the details which you given are informative. 

I’ve been to this national park in this year which month aaheemmm March. 

The Long lake was frozen and the mountains had snows on them as if those cuties were wearing a fluffy cap 😂 that was my top favorite stop , then panda lake then fiver colored one. 

It was one day trip so I was able to see only 3-4 spots. 

Yeah but it was really cool place to relax and take photos. 

❤❤❤❤❤❤❤

author: @noms780
text:
Photographer here. Thanks for sharing your experience! Your footage looks great and overcast light is actually quite nice for color and atmosphere.  Was this video uploaded at the highest quality 4k?

I am also planning to spend two days here in late October and was wondering about the random drop off point in the morning. If I wanted to visit Grass and Swan lakes, assume it is always possible to get off at Shuzeng then take another bus from the designated bus stop (to Virgin forest), then walk down to the lakes? Another concern was whether these lakes would be in shadow if I visited too early.

author: @yonathanchristanto9968
text: Hello, I’d like to ask whether it’s better to visit Jiuzhaigou with a tour (from Chengdu) or go independently. If I go independently, is it easy to arrange transportation, food, and hotels? Also, may I know the total cost you spent on your trip there?

text: Did you go in the weekend and what dates in September? We were planing to go in early September in a weekday to avoid crowds ?

text:
Hi Cherie, thank you for this lovely
and tasteful video.  Could visitors experience breathing difficulties at this high altitude and is there a remedy such as oxygen bottles available?  2) Which is the lowest place in this park and are hotels available there?  3) Are there scenic areas below 10,000 feet?  4) Where can I find a map which shows the various routes for walks as well as their altitudes?  5) Is there a quiet (less crowded) time to visit apart from winter? Thank you very much.  Best wishes to you and thanks again and hope I can receive a reply although it is a long time since this video was posted.

author: @ariciptagunawan
text:
Hi there, Cherie. Great video! 

I was there in November visiting both Huanglong and Jiuzhaigou. They were breathtaking and will definitely be coming back to Jiuzhaigou in a different season. Anyway, I just saw your blog and was about to ask whether we can stay in an accommodation inside the park, but apparently you were staying outside and bought two tickets, right? Also, I was a bit startled seeing the price because mine was RMB160. Different tourism season. 😅

author: @lavenderlavender4985
text: I wonder if anyone has visited during June before? Is it very hot and rainy?

author: @peterscrace3530
text: Excellent video, China really is a beautiful country and nothing like what the western media make it out to be, I have been to China many many times and this place is certainly on my to do list. That said Chinese people are very hospitable, friendly, curious and helpful on a one on one basis however when there is a crowd I have found Chinese people to be inconsiderate, impatient and rude at least by Australian standards, something I have never gotten used to. If you enjoyed this place I highly recommend Yunnan Province for its lakes and water falls, absolutely breath taking.
